evals.prompt: Create an operations readiness plan for a 100MW Tier III hyperscale data center with self-operated 24/7 staffing. The facility has 4 power trains, 3 cooling plants, and 20 IT halls. Commissioning (Level 4 IST) completes in 3 months. The operations team is a new build (no existing team). Self-operated with no managed services provider. Single tenant (cloud provider). The facility manager wants to have Day 1 readiness when IST completes.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive ops readiness plan with 50-70 FTE staffing model (CFEs, electrical/mechanical techs, NOC operators, security, admin). 24/7 shift coverage with 4-shift rotation. 155-210 SOPs prioritized by safety criticality (P1 emergency procedures first). Critical spares list with $5-10M estimated inventory including MV breakers (26-52 week lead time). 4-severity incident response with escalation matrix to CEO/CTO. Day 1/30/90 milestones. Recruiting timeline starting immediately (90-120 day CFE time-to-fill before IST completes in 3 months).
assertions: Staffing model provides 50-70 FTE with role breakdown and shift coverage for 24/7 operations
CFE recruiting timeline starts immediately given 90-120 day time-to-fill vs 3-month IST completion
SOP count is 155-210 with P1 safety-critical procedures required on Day 1
Spare parts strategy includes MV breakers with 26-52 week lead time flagged
Day 1/Day 30/Day 90 milestones are specified with concrete checklist items
Incident response includes 4 severity levels with escalation matrix and notification requirements

evals.prompt: Develop an ops readiness plan for a 20MW Tier III colo facility transitioning from construction to a managed services provider (MSP) for operations. The MSP contract covers 24/7 facility operations, NOC monitoring, and routine maintenance. The owner retains asset management, capital decisions, and tenant relations. 30 tenants expected at stabilization. The MSP requires a 60-day overlap with the construction team for knowledge transfer. Commissioning completes in 6 weeks.
evals.expected_output: An ops readiness plan addressing the MSP handover with 60-day overlap period, knowledge transfer milestones, facility-specific SOP development (not generic MSP procedures), 22-28 FTE MSP team sizing, tenant-facing operations planning (5 FTE for 30 tenants), SLA reporting framework for monthly tenant reports, spare parts inventory handover, vendor contract assignment from developer to MSP. Day 1 focuses on MSP operational readiness verification.
assertions: 60-day MSP overlap period with construction team is planned with knowledge transfer milestones
Facility-specific SOPs are required (not generic MSP procedures)
Tenant-facing operations staff (5 FTE for 30 tenants) is budgeted beyond engineering team
SLA reporting framework for monthly tenant reports is specified
Vendor service contract assignment from developer to MSP is addressed
Day 1 checklist includes MSP operational readiness verification

evals.prompt: Create an ops readiness plan for a fleet of 5 modular data centers (2MW each, 10MW total) with minimal on-site staff and centralized remote monitoring from a regional NOC. Each site is Tier II with business-hours on-site staffing and 24/7 remote monitoring. Staff rotate between sites. One facility manager covers all 5 sites. Commissioning is staggered (one site per month over 5 months). Budget is constrained -- operations cost must stay under $2M annually.
evals.expected_output: A lean ops readiness plan with 8-10 FTE covering all 5 sites (mobile technicians rotating between sites, centralized NOC operators). Staggered commissioning handover plan (one site per month). Simplified SOP set (60-85 per site, standardized across fleet). Minimal spare parts (shared pool across 5 sites with mobile spares van). Remote monitoring architecture with alarm escalation to NOC. Incident response adapted for remote response (30-60 min dispatch time). Annual operations cost estimate under $2M.
assertions: Total FTE of 8-10 covers all 5 sites with mobile technician rotation model
Staggered commissioning handover plan addresses one site per month rollout
Standardized SOPs across fleet reduce development effort (write once, deploy 5x)
Shared spare parts pool across 5 sites reduces inventory investment
Remote monitoring with NOC alarm escalation addresses unmanned hours
Annual operations cost estimate demonstrates staying under $2M budget constraint
